message:
refactor: doctor dog reports structured data, removes hardcoded thresholds (gt-0cxc)

The doctor dog was making judgment calls (threshold comparisons, escalations,
server restarts, zombie kills) that violate ZFC — Go transports data, agents
decide. Now it collects metrics and writes a structured JSON report to
.doctor-dog-report.json for agent consumption.

Removed:
- Hardcoded threshold constants (latency, DB count, backup age, disk size)
- All d.escalate() calls from doctor dog checks
- Server restart logic (doctorDogRestartServer)
- Zombie kill logic
- MaxDBCount config field

Added:
- DoctorDogReport and sub-types for structured metric data
- JSON report written to {TownRoot}/.doctor-dog-report.json each cycle
- Report includes: TCP reachability, latency, database list, GC results,
  zombie processes, backup ages, disk usage

Kept:
- GC execution (maintenance, not judgment)
- All operational timeouts (TCP, query, GC)
- Metric collection logic (same checks, just report instead of judge)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
